figures.caption: Figure 4: Comparison of one-step updates for DDPM and q-sampling. For each plotted timestep t, we run DDPM from t′ = T to t′ = t. Then, we divide the trajectories into correct and incorrect based on x̂θ(xt). The next step t′ = t − 1 is obtained with either DDPM or q-sampling. The plots show (a) correctness of predictions x̂θ(xt−1), (b) mean distance ∥xt − xt−1∥, (c) mean density p(xt−1), (d) mean magnitude of predictions ∥x̂θ(xt−1)∥.

Figure 9: Quality metrics for TEncDM-based models without self-conditining (SC) for different q-sampling activation timesteps and varying amount of generation steps on ROCStories and Wikipedia. We use the baseline SC model with p = 0.5.

Figure 10: Quality metrics for TEncDM-based models with self-conditining (SC) for different q-sampling activation timesteps and varying amount of generation steps on ROCStories and Wikipedia. We use the baseline SC model with p = 0.5.
Table 5: Four MBR candidates and the chosen sample for different activation timesteps tact.
Table 10: Impact of q-sampling (QS), self-conditioning, and MBR on image diffusion on CIFAR-10.

Figure 13: Qualitative comparison on CIFAR-10. Generation is initialized from the same seeds. The first column corresponds to MBR-selected samples, while the last column corresponds to q-sampling (QS). As can be seen, q-sampling produces significantly smoother images.

Figure 1: Toy visualization of sampling with continuous diffusion models in continuous and discrete data domains. Orange contours visualize the density of noisified data p(xt). Blue points and arrows show in-distribution sampling trajectories, red — out-of-distribution trajectories. White diamonds show original data points in the discrete setting.

Figure 5: (a) mean distance between predictions ∥x̂θ(xt)− x̂θ(xt−1)∥ for correct and incorrect trajectories without SC and for the optimal model; (b) mean distance between predictions ∥x̂θ(xt) − x̂θ(xt−1)∥ with and without SC; (c) correctness along the trajectory with and without SC. Gray vertical stripes show the transition interval.
Figure 7: Quality metrics with and without self-conditoning (SC) for different q-sampling activation timestep on ROCStories and Wikipedia. We use the baseline SC model with p = 0.5.
Figure 8: Quality metrics with and without self-conditoning (SC) for different q-sampling activation timestep on OpenWebText. We use the baseline SC model with p = 0.5.
Figure 11: Quality metrics with and without self-conditoning (SC) for different q-sampling activation timestep on SwissProt protein generation. We use the baseline SC model with p = 0.5.
Table 1: Impact of q-sampling (QS) and self-conditioning (SC) on ROCStories and Wikipedia. SC trained with probability p is denoted SCp; QS with optimal tact is denoted QStact/T .

Figure 2: (a) correctness of predictions x̂θ(xt) and (b) mean probability density p(xt) along correct, incorrect and optimal sampling trajectories; (c) share of pairs of trajectories lying within the same mode (separately for all learned and optimal trajectories). Gray vertical stripes show the transition interval.
Table 3: Max sequence lengths used for each dataset.

Figure 14: Correctness (left axis, blue) and diversity (right axis, green) as functions of the q-sampling activation time tact, shown for the three patch-based CIFAR-10 experiments. Solid lines correspond to the base model, dashed lines to self-conditioning (SC).
Figure 6: XSum (top) and CoNaLa (bottom) quality for varying q-sampling activation timestep with and without SC.

Table 4: Five MBR candidates and chosen samples for different activation timesteps tact. We use latin letters to represent each token. Correct sequences are highlighted in green and incorrect in red. Activating MBR at tact/T = 0.3 or tact/T = 0.5 ensures that the correct sequence is sampled; this would not be the case without MBR.

Figure 3: left: ratio of candidate sets S with at least one correct sample vs. its size |S| for different activation timesteps tact; right: correctness and diversity of MBR with |S| = 5, self-conditioning (SC), and q-sampling vs. their activation timestep tact. Gray vertical stripes show the transition interval.
